title: Today, Senate Minority Leader Brian W. Jones ( R-San Diego ) warned Californians to prepare for unpredictable and fast-rising fuel prices as two major state-imposed gas cost increa
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: California gas prices could soar 75% to $8.43 per gallon by 2026, driven by refinery shutdowns and the state’s overreach with excessive regulation and taxation.
The Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) regulations could raise gas prices by 65 cents per gallon.
The state gas tax has reached 61.2 cents per gallon, the highest gas tax in the nation.
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) has imposed additional costs in the fuel supply chain.
State-imposed costs will lead to unpredictable spikes in fuel prices throughout the summer and the foreseeable future.
